name: Entity Teleport
desc: Teleports an entity to an absolute position (fixed-point int × 32) and rotation.

fields.name: onGround
fields.desc: Whether the entity is on ground

notes: Unlike S14 (Entity Relative Move), S18 sets ABSOLUTE position — the entity moves to this exact location regardless of where it was. Used when players teleport (ender pearls, chorus fruit), mobs teleport (endermen), or when S14 cumulative errors need correction.

implementation.overview: S18PacketEntityTeleport sets an entity's absolute position. Unlike S14PacketEntity (relative move with byte offsets), S18 sets exact coordinates. Essential for KillAura (update target position for aim prediction), ESP (sync entity render position), and Backtrack modules (record S18 teleports for replay). Backtrack (Aqua 13 / Rise 6.0) exempts S18 from delay buffer because teleport packets must be processed immediately to avoid entity position desync.

modules.name: KillAura / ESP Position Sync
modules.purpose: Update the tracked entity's position when S18 arrives, ensuring KillAura aim prediction and ESP rendering use the correct entity location.
modules.how_it_works: When S18 arrives, the module extracts entityId and absolute coordinates (x/32.0, y/32.0, z/32.0). It updates the entity's position in the KillAura target list and ESP render cache. For aim prediction, the teleport position is used as a reference point for calculating future movement vectors.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleEntityTeleport(S18PacketEntityTeleport) — finds Entity by ID in WorldClient, calls setPositionAndRotation2() for interpolated movement.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: S18 position tracking is passive and undetectable. Backtrack modules that exempt S18 from their delay buffer prevent teleport desync — if a teleport is delayed, the entity appears to move back to the teleport position after a delay (rubberbanding), which is noticeable by other players but not flagged by anti-cheats.

modules.name: Backtrack Exemption (Aqua 13 / Rise 6.0)
modules.purpose: Exempt S18PacketEntityTeleport from the Backtrack replay buffer. Teleport packets must be processed immediately — delaying them causes entities to appear in the wrong position.
modules.how_it_works: Backtrack buffers all incoming packets for a configurable delay (replaying them for position/rotation interpolation). Teleport packets (S18) are exempted because they represent absolute position resets, not relative movement. If S18 is delayed, the entity appears in its old position for the delay period then suddenly teleports.
modules.vanilla_hook: EventPacket — Backtrack intercepts incoming packets but lets S18 bypass its buffer.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: N/A — Backtrack delaying S18 would cause visual rubberbanding, not anti-cheat flags.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleEntityTeleport(S18PacketEntityTeleport) — sets entity absolute position. All major clients fire PacketReceiveEvent.

Server: EntityTrackerEntry sends S18 when an entity teleports (ender pearl, enderman TP, commands), or when S14 cumulative position error exceeds a threshold.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: KillAura tracks target position from S18. Backtrack exempts S18 from delay.
Sigma 4.11: ESP/KillAura reads S18 for entity positions.
Tenacity 6.0: KillAura tracks S18 for aim prediction.
Lycoris 2.0: KillAura target tracking from S18.
Aqua 13: Backtrack exempts S18 from delay buffer.
